g.text: Write Bash/Python manually, Google + Stack Overflow
grep logs, SSH, guess → fix → test loop
Trivy, Snyk CLI — manual review of output
Build Jenkinsfile/YAML from scratch every time
Jenkins, Terraform, kubectl, bash, AWS CLI

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I use GitHub Copilot or Claude for shell scripting?')
g.text: Copilot/Claude generates scripts; you review + fix

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How can AI help me write Terraform code?')
g.text: AI drafts modules; engineer validates + customises

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is AI used for incident response in DevOps?')
g.text: Dynatrace Davis AI, PagerDuty AIOps auto-triage

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is AI used in DevSecOps and container scanning?')
g.text: Snyk AI, Wiz — auto-prioritise + suggest fixes

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does AI help with DevOps documentation?')
g.text: AI generates runbooks, READMEs from code/infra

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How can AI speed up building CI/CD pipelines?')
g.text: AI suggests pipeline YAML; you tune + secure it

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is AI used for log analysis in DevOps?')
sendPrompt('What new tools should a DevOps engineer learn because of AI?')
g.text: + Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q

svg.text: Key shift: engineers now spend less time writing, more time reviewing + validating AI output
Linux + AWS fundamentals remain critical — AI cannot replace deep system knowledge
